news.title:
🇿🇦 elon musk blasts south africa's race laws:

"there are now more anti-white and anti-asian laws in
news.summary:
🇿🇦 elon musk blasts south africa's race laws:

"there are now more anti-white and anti-asian laws in south africa than anti-black laws under apartheid. racism is wrong no matter who it is against."

apartheid ended more than 30 years ago, but today south africa has 145 race-based laws.

more than ever before.

the government claims these laws are necessary to correct “past injustice.” 

in other words, they are fighting racism in the past with racism in the present.

laws that discriminate against white people are legal in south africa because the constitution permits so-called “fair” discrimination. 

"fair" discrimination is 디파이ned as any discrimination that furthers “equality.”

to quote directly from the 'equality' clause of the constitution:

“discrimination on one or more of the listed grounds is unfair unless it is established that the discrimination is fair.”

news.title:
🇨🇴 russian-born mayor of the colombian city of tunja dismissed over corruption charges

colombia’s c
news.summary:
🇨🇴 russian-born mayor of the colombian city of tunja dismissed over corruption charges

colombia’s council of state has upheld the decision to annul the election of tunja’s mayor. the position was won in 2023 by mikhail krasnov, a native of saratov, russia.

a criminal corruption case has been opened against the former mayor and three members of his administration. the defendants are accused of usurping public functions and abuse of office.

krasnov moved to colombia in 2008 as an 거래소 student. before his election, the russian had been working as an economics professor at a local university. 

among locals, the mayor was affectionately known as “el ruso” (“the russian”) and “el profesor” (“the professor”).

news.title:
risk appetite among institutional investors is skyrocketing:

institutional investors purchased ~22%
news.summary:
risk appetite among institutional investors is skyrocketing:

institutional investors purchased ~22% more call than put options last week, the largest difference in at least 16 months, according to citadel.

monday and tuesday also saw significant institutional call option inflows, now on track for the largest weekly bullish reading since october 2024.

as a result, institutional demand for call options is up to the highest level on record.

institutional call option purchases over 5 days ending tuesday have exceeded the average since january 2025 by +37%.

by comparison, during the april 2025 market recovery, the average was surpassed by +25%.

institutional investors are piling into equities.

the 10th and final step of our "conflict playbook" is on the horizon and the mar
news.summary:
there it is folks:

the 10th and final step of our "conflict playbook" is on the horizon and the market is surging as expected.

and now, not only has the ai revolution rapidly accelerated in the background of this 7-week war, but markets are pricing-in an 인플레이션 shock.

those who SOLd assets in march are rushing back into the market as quality names have only gotten cheaper on a forward p/e basis, risk appetite is returning, and the search for yield is back on.

couple this with one of the largest short squeezes in years, and the S&P 500 has now added nearly +$7 trillion in ~3 weeks.

own assets or be left behind.

news.title: 속보: the us is considering a "$20 billion cash-for-uranium deal" with iran as part of a new "3 page p
news.summary:
속보: the us is considering a "$20 billion cash-for-uranium deal" with iran as part of a new "3 page peace plan," per axios.

details include:

1. the us and iran are negotiating over a 3-page plan to end the war

2. one element under discussion is that the us would release $20 billion in frozen iranian funds in return for iran giving up its stockpile of enriched uranium

3. some of iran's highly enriched uranium would be shipped to a third country and some would be down-blended in iran under international monitoring

4. iran would be allowed to have nuclear research reactors for the production of medical isotopes, but would pledge that all of its nuclear facilities would be above ground

a second round of us-iran talks is expected in pakistan on sunday.

news.title:
global gold demand is being primarily driven by emerging markets:

emerging markets have accounted f
news.summary:
global gold demand is being primarily driven by emerging markets:

emerging markets have accounted for 70% of world gold demand over the last 10 years.

greater china alone made up 27% of global demand, the largest portion of any region, followed by india at 21%.

as a result, the two countries accounted for nearly half of all global gold demand.

by comparison, north america and europe, including russia, represented just 11% and 12%, respectively.

on the supply side, mine production accounted for 74% of total gold supply, with recycling reflecting the remaining 26%.

africa is the largest gold supply source, at 26%, followed by asia at 19%, the commonwealth of independent states (cis), central, and south america at 15% each, and north america at 14%.

emerging markets are fueling the gold rush.

news.title:
에센피랑 하이일드 크레딧 스프레드랑 저렇게 갭이 떴어.. 

에센피는 다시 빠르게 반등해서 ath 찍었는데.. 
크레딧은 반등폭을 못 따라가고 있는게 찜찜한 부분이야.  

저거
news.summary:
에센피랑 하이일드 크레딧 스프레드랑 저렇게 갭이 떴어.. 

에센피는 다시 빠르게 반등해서 ath 찍었는데.. 
크레딧은 반등폭을 못 따라가고 있는게 찜찜한 부분이야.  

저거 흰색선을 쉽게 설명해주자면,  
cdx hy cdsi gen 5y 가 뭐냐면, 미국 하이일드(정크) 기업들의 신용위험을 보여주는 대표 cds 지수 (5년물) 이야.  

그래서 스프레드가 확대될 수록, 기업들의 부도 리스크도 올라가고 금융 스트레스가 올라가고, 경기도 나쁜거지.  물론 여기 차트에서는 에센피랑 비교를 위해서 inverted 로 표기해서 반대로 해석해야 되고.  

그러니까 크레딧도 조금 개선되긴 했는데, 에센피가 지금 평소대비 혼자 너무 오바해서 슈팅하고 있다는 소리야.

news.title: this is amy biehl; a bright, compassionate 26-year-old american who went to south africa in 1993 to
news.summary:
this is amy biehl; a bright, compassionate 26-year-old american who went to south africa in 1993 to help fight apartheid and support democracy.

while driving through a township near cape town, she was pulled from her car by a mob chanting “kill the settler!” and “one settler, one bullet.” 

she was stoned, beaten, and stabbed to death in a frenzied racial attack simply because she was white.
